title: Animate provider cards during cross-CLI interaction
summary: Translate new ring-bus sender and recipient identities into a short-lived, accessible interaction pulse on the matching provider cards.
risks: Broadcast messages could animate unrelated cards
Persistent terminal motion could distract users
Anonymous mode could expose session identity

complexity.need_now: Make live collaboration visible on the existing provider cards without adding another panel or daemon.
complexity.simplest_viable_design: Keep a short-lived set of provider IDs in the TUI, derive it from new bus messages, and render a deterministic card pulse with a static reduced-motion state.
evidence: The TUI already polls ring growth every tick
Provider cards already redraw on a 500ms animation tick
Bus identities begin with provider CLI names

rejected_options.reason: The existing TUI tick already observes ring growth and can own ephemeral presentation state
That would imply interaction recipients not proven by the message

reversibility.rollback: Remove the ephemeral interaction map and card interaction rendering parameters.

tests: Unit: bus identities map only to supported provider IDs
Unit: interaction chrome changes deterministically and has a reduced-motion state
Regression: full TUI and parser test suite remains green
